observations.recommendation:
In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management
agreed with the following:
1.11.1. Ensure that the Inventory Committee prepare a complete
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) which shall be reconciled with the PPE records kept
by the General Services Office and Accounting Office;
1.11.2. Reconcile the RPCPPE and the ARE/PAR and ensure that the
said ARE/PAR be renewed every after three years pursuant to
Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish effective
management and control over PPE;
1.11.3. The General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant
reconcile their property records, and also reconcile the same
with the results of the physical count to ensure that the PPE
recorded in the property records actually exist and all existing
PPEs are recorded in the books, in order to establish
correctness and completeness of the balances of PPE
accounts. Any variance should be handled accordingly and
subsequently adjusted if the need arises;
1.11.4. The Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of
unserviceable PPEs, if any.

In view of the noted deficiencies, we recommended and Management
agreed with the following:
2.5.1. The Municipal Accountant prepare and/or submit a copy of the
subsidiary records for roads and road components for every
road network, and the lapsing schedule for the computation of
the depreciation for each component to verify the computation
of the depreciation and the net value of the road network;
2.5.2. That the Inventory Committee submit a revised and duly-signed
Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network;
2.5.3. Henceforth invite the audit team to witness the inventory-taking
of road network; and
2.5.4. Adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed
that –
3.3.5.1. Submit a written request to the bank for the debit memo of
the charges made against the LGU’s account; and
3.3.5.2. Henceforth, ensure that the entries made are duly attached
with supporting documents.

In view of the noted deficiencies, we recommended and Management
agreed with the following:
4.8.1. That the Municipal Accountant –
4.8.1.1. Analyze the transaction and adjust the books of accounts
accordingly;
4.8.1.2. Provide adequate disclosure on biological assets in the
Notes to Financial Statements, such as, but not limited to,
the following:
(a) Basis of measurement or value recorded in the books;
(b) Description of the biological assets; and
(c) The program under which such biological assets were
purchased and its purpose.
4.8.1.3. Maintain Ledger Card for the Breeding Stock;
4.8.1.4. Coordinate with the Municipal Agriculture Office for
dispersal program under their office, and require
submission of duly accomplished acknowledgement
receipt for proper accounting of animals in the books.
4.8.2. That the Municipal Agriculturist –
4.8.2.1. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and submit a
status report and duly accomplished acknowledgment
receipt on the dispersal program of their office;
4.8.2.2. Maintain a Livestock Card for each type of large cattle and
other bovines acquired thru purchase or raised, as well as
for small animals including fowls, as provided under the
Handbook on Property and Supply and Management
System; and
4.8.2.3. Reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal
Accountant to determine the differences and to inform the
Accountant of the raised animals that reached the
marketable age or are ready for use.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended that Management exhaust
all effort to establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts,
and furnish COA with a copy of relevant records containing sufficient
information about the foregoing receivable balances in order to effect
collection thereof, or file request for authority to write-off dormant
receivable accounts to COA following the procedures stated in COA
Circular No. 2016-005.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended that Management exhaust
all effort to establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts,
and furnish COA with a copy of relevant records containing sufficient
information about the foregoing receivable balances in order to effect
collection thereof, or file request for authority to write-off dormant
receivable accounts to COA following the procedures stated in COA
Circular No. 2016-005.

In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended and Management
agreed that–
7.9.1. Issue letters of demand to the Implementing Agencies for the
liquidation of the funds transferred and furnish the Audit Team
of the said letters;
7.9.2. Require the Implementing Agencies to submit the Report of
Checks Issued and the Report of Disbursements and present a
copy of which for audit; and
7.9.3. Henceforth, abide with provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013
and COA Circular No. 2012-001 regarding the fund transfers.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed
the following:
8.9.1. On the grant of the cash advance –
8.9.1.1. The required supporting documents and the Municipal
Accountant’s certification are attached before grant of the
cash advance; and
8.9.1.2. Review the documentary requirements discussed in COA
Circular No. 2012-001, and other relevant rules and
regulations.
8.9.2. On the liquidation of the cash advance –
8.9.2.1. The Municipal Treasurer refund the unexpended balance
of cash advances at the end of the year or as soon as the
purpose has been served as prescribed under
regulations;
8.9.2.2. The Municipal Accountant regularly prepare and submit
the Report of Disbursements with duly accomplished
vouchers/payrolls and supporting documents to the
Accountant for timely recording in the books of accounts
for fair presentation of the financial statements.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed to:
9.6.1. Ensure that the RPT Receivables and SET Receivables be
supported with list of tax payers, pursuant to Section 20 of
NGAS to establish the correctness of the collectibles; and
9.6.2. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor to establish
correctness of the collectible real property and determine any
changes/updates in the real properties.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed to –
10.6.1. Submit the Certified List of All Real Property Tax
Delinquencies and the List of Tax Delinquent for more than five
years, if any, and reconcile the same with the accounting
records; and
10.6.2. Formulate additional strategies to intensify and facilitate tax
collections. If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section
174 of R.A. No. 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the
municipal government in order to increase the collection
efficiency.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed to:
11.15.1. Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to
immediately submit the above stated lacking documents for
the audit team to evaluate and establish the validity of the
disbursements; and
11.15.2. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer should effect payments
only on vouchers that are properly documented and instruct
the Municipal Accountant to prepare and attach checklist of
documentary requirements for every transaction/DV and
ensure that all required documents were submitted.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management
agreed to ensure the submission of accounts and financial statements
within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No.
7160, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01
and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006. Monthly accounts should
be submitted along with the journals and financial statements and trial
balances to facilitate audit and verification.

We recommended and Management agreed that the BAC Chairperson
ensure the submission of government contracts within the reglementary
period set forth in Item 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-01 for a
systematic and effective review process to come up with timely and
relevant audit results.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere with the transparency
provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

In view of the noted lapses, we recommended and Management agreed to
abide with the rules mandated by the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 that
governs the Small Value Procurement under the Negotiated Procurement.

In view of the above observation, we recommended and Management agreed
submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team for proper evaluation. Henceforth,
design and implement controls on the submission of complete documentation
before proceeding to payment as adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-001 to avoid
recurrence of such observation and suspension of agency transactions.

In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended Management
agreed to henceforth –
17.11.1. Ensure that observers from COA and other non-related
organizations were invited to witness all stages of the bidding
process to enhance transparency; and
17.11.2. Adhere to the guidelines to the rules and regulations set forth
in the Updated 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184, including
latest applicable GPPB issuances in the conduct of public
bidding for government procurements to enhance
transparency and assure that projects are legally awarded to
the most favorable and qualified bidders.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to the provisions of the
Government Procurement Reform Act and its Implementing Rules and
Regulations.

19.5.9. We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the
existence of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of
R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence, before
transactions are made or contracts are entered into by the
Municipality.

20.4.12. We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the
existence of an appropriation in accordance with Section 7 of
R.A. No. 7160 and the afore-cited jurisprudence, before
transactions are made or contracts are entered into by the
Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
1.9 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
1.9.1 Management create an Inventory Committee that will perform the
conduct of comprehensive annual physical count of PPE in
accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume
1. Also, a representative of the Audit Team should be invited to
witness the inventory taking for this purpose;
1.9.2 Management ensure that the Inventory Committee prepare the
Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) which shall be reconciled with the PPE records kept by the
General Services Office and Accounting Office;
1.9.3 The General Services Officer reconcile the Property Cards for PPE
and the ARE/PAR and ensure that the said ARE/PAR be renewed
every after three years pursuant to Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1,
to establish effective management and control over PPE;
1.9.4 The General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile
their property records with the results of the physical count to ensure
that the PPE recorded in the property records actually exist and all
existing PPEs are recorded in the books, in order to establish
correctness and completeness of the balances of PPE accounts. Any
variance should be handled accordingly and subsequently adjusted if
the need arises;
1.9.5 The Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of
unserviceable PPEs, if any.

2.9 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
2.9.1 That the Inventory Committee conduct a yearly physical count of local
road network projects and submit to this office the necessary reports
and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
2.9.2 That the Municipal Accountant keep and maintain a subsidiary ledger
for roads and its components and reconcile the same with the result of
the physical inventory-taking at the end of the year.

3.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile all
available records and documents, exhaust all efforts to determine the details
of the CIP – Buildings and Other Structures account balance and prepare
adjusting entries, if necessary, to come up with a more accurate and reliable
CIP account balance.

4.8 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
That the Municipal Accountant –
4.8.1 Analyze the transaction and adjust the books of accounts accordingly;
4.8.2 Provide adequate disclosure on biological assets in the Notes to
Financial Statements, such as, but not limited to, the following:
(a) Bases of measurement or value recorded in the books;
(b) Description of the biological assets; and
(c) The program under which such biological assets were
purchased and its purpose.
4.8.3 Maintain Ledger Card for the Breeding Stock;
4.8.4 Coordinate with the Municipal Agriculture Office for dispersal
program under their office, and require submission of duly
accomplished acknowledgement receipt for proper accounting of
animals in the books.
That the Municipal Agriculturist –
4.8.5 Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and submit a status report
and duly accomplished acknowledgment receipt on the dispersal
program of their office;
4.8.6 Maintain a Livestock Card for each type of large cattle and other
bovines acquired thru purchase or raised, as well as for small animals
including fowls, as provided under the Handbook on Property and
Supply and Management System; and
4.8.7 Reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal Accountant
to determine the differences and to inform the Accountant of the
raised animals that reached the marketable age or are ready for use.

5.6 In view of the noted lapses and deficiencies, we have recommended the
following:
5.6.1 That the Municipal Budget Officer coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in the preparation of the
reconciliation statement of SCBAA;
5.6.3 That the Municipal Mayor instruct the Department Heads to strictly
adhere to the budgetary and disbursement process; and
5.6.4 Henceforth, the Management review the various budgetary,
disbursement, and accounting processes and ensure the strict
compliance thereof.

6.3 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
6.3.1 That the Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant
with the necessary supporting documents that warrant a Journal
Entry Voucher and General Journal entry; and
6.3.2 That the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry
Voucher, attached with the necessary supporting documents, as a
basis of recording in the general Journal.

7.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the following
adjusting entry to rectify the noted error:
Account
Account Code Debit Credit

Guaranty/Security Deposits

To adjust security deposits from contracts erroneously recorded as Bail
Bonds Payable.

8.5 We have recommended that Management –
8.5.1 Submit a written request to the bank for the debit memo of the charges
made against the LGU’s account; and
8.5.2 Henceforth, ensure that the entries made are duly attached with
supporting documents.

9.5 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that Management
coordinate with the contractors to facilitate the refund of their security
deposits; and henceforth, properly monitor the Guaranty Deposits Payable
account and refund the amount due to the contractors.

10.8 We have recommended that Management exhaust all effort to establish the
existence and validity of the receivable accounts, and furnish COA with a
copy of relevant records containing sufficient information about the
foregoing receivable balances in order to effect collection thereof, or file
request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA
following the procedures stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

existence and validity of the receivable accounts, and furnish COA with a
copy of relevant records containing sufficient information about the
foregoing receivable balances in order to effect collection thereof, or file
request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA
following the procedures stated in COA Circular No. 2016-005.

12.6 We have recommended the following:
12.6.1That the official and employees immediately liquidate and/or refund
the cash advances, and issue an official receipt for the refunded
amount; and
12.6.2 Henceforth, abide with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002
and other relevant laws and regulations regarding the proper
liquidation of the cash advances.

13.6 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that the Municipal
Accountant:
• Ensure that the set-up of RPT Receivables and SET Receivables be
established at the beginning of the year and that the amount of
receivables should be supported with list of tax payers, pursuant to
Section 20 of NGAS to establish the correctness of the collectibles; and
• Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor
to establish correctness of the collectible real property and determine
any changes/updates in the real properties.

14.6 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that Management -
14.6.1 Submit the Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies
and the List of Tax Delinquent for more than five years, if any, and
reconcile the same with the accounting records; and
14.6.2 Formulate additional strategies to intensify and facilitate tax
collections. If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of
R.A. No. 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal
government in order to increase the collection efficiency.

15.7 We have recommended that Management:
• Provide for audit the documents supporting the said accountability of
the former Municipal Treasurer; and
• Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit the cash in
treasury balance pertaining to her accountability to the authorized
depository bank of the LGU and furnish a copy of the deposit slip to the
audit team. Henceforth, ensure to deposit all collections intact and
within the period prescribed in the above-cited provisions.

16.4 We have recommended that:
16.4.1The required supporting documents and the Municipal Accountant’s
certification are attached before grant of the cash advance; and
16.4.2 That Management review the documentary requirements discussed
in COA Circular No. 2012-001, and other relevant rules and
regulations.

17.7 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
• That the Municipal Treasurer refund the unexpended balance of cash
advances at the end of the year or as soon as the purpose has been
served as prescribed under regulations;
• That the Municipal Accountant effect the adjustments to reflect the
correct amount of liquidations and to rectify the balance of the
receivables; and
• That the Municipal Accountant regularly prepare and submit the
Report of Disbursements with duly accomplished vouchers/payrolls
and supporting documents to the Accountant for timely recording in
the books of accounts for fair presentation of the financial statements.

18.8 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended that Management
–
18.8.1 Issue letters of demand to the Implementing Agencies for the
liquidation of the funds transferred and furnish the Audit Team of
the said letters;
18.8.2 Require the Implementing Agencies to submit the Report of Checks
Issued and the Report of Disbursements and present a copy of which
for audit; and
18.8.3 Henceforth, abide with provisions of COA Circular No. 94-013 and
COA Circular No. 2012-001 regarding the fund transfers.

19.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records, and
immediately remit any noted discrepancies based on the reconciliation.
Henceforth, strictly comply with the Implementing Rules and Regulations
of Republic Act No. 10606.

20.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records, and
immediately remit noted discrepancies, if any. Henceforth, strictly comply
with the Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 8291.

21.5 We have recommended that Management instruct the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records, and
remit noted discrepancies, if any, based on their reconciliation. Henceforth,
strictly comply with the Implementing Guidelines of Republic Act No. 9679.

22.7 We have recommended that all Memorandum of Agreements (MOA)
entered into by the Municipality and Notice of Authority to Debit Account
Issued (NADAI) be submitted to the audit team for verification.
Henceforth, ensure that funds received from National Government
Agencies and all other sources are covered by Memorandum of Agreement
and that the same shall be submitted to COA within 5 days from perfection
thereof for audit purposes.

23.7 We have recommended that Management instruct the BAC Chairperson to
revisit the PhilGEPS website and effect necessary adjustments as to the
status of the contracts closed in the website, and submit to COA all
perfected contracts and purchase orders for the immediate review of the
documents and, henceforth, be guided on the submission of the same as
prescribed by COA Circular No. 2009-001.

24.5 We have recommended that Management –
24.5.1 Submit the APP and supporting PPMPs for CY 2021 for audit
purposes; and
24.5.2 Always prepare APP and supporting PPMPs which serve as a basis
guide on their procurement activities pursuant to Section 7 of the
Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184. A copy thereof be furnished to COA
for audit purposes.

25.9 We have recommended the Management to adhere with the transparency
provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

25.10 We have further recommended that Management immediately submit the
list of all implemented and on-going projects of the Municipality for CY
2021.

26.5 We have recommended the Management to submit the lacking documents
to support the disbursements, and henceforth, adhere to COA Circular No.
2012-001 for complete documentation of common government
transactions.

27.6 We have recommended that Management create and maintain separate
depository bank accounts for its shares from RA 7171 and RA 8240, as well
as, prepare the required quarterly status report to facilitate proper
monitoring on the implementation and utilizations of the funds.

28.4 We have recommended that Management ensure the proper
implementation of PPAs included in the budget to address the issues,
concerns and needs of tobacco farmers in the locality.

29.3 We have recommended that Management ensure proper planning and
budgeting of projects, programs and activities for RA 7171 fund to properly
address the issues, concerns and needs of tobacco farmers of the locality.

30.5 We have recommended that Management submit copies of MOA for the
noted contracts, if any. Henceforth, enter into agreement with
implementing agencies to determine the purpose of future projects intended
for transfer and/or distribution.

31.7 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended that Management
31.7.1Have a more thorough planning and ensure that the PPAs can be
implemented in order to deliver the benefits for the intended
recipients; and
31.7.2Attach the three-year program to the LSB Budget.

32.7 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that Management abide
with the rules mandated by the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 that governs
the Small Value Procurement under the Negotiated Procurement.

33.6 We have recommended that Management prepare and/or submit the
approved APP and its supporting PPMPs for audit purposes. Henceforth,
attach copies of approved APP and PPMPs to all procurement transactions
to serve as guide in the procurement activities of the LGU.

34.4 We have recommended that Management, henceforth, properly allocate
budget for SCPWD Fund to address the issues and concerns of the elderly
and persons with disabilities of the locality.

35.6 We have recommended that Management ensure the proper planning and
budgeting of programs, projects and activities for the SCPWD fund to
address the issues and concerns of the SCPWD organization and members
of the municipality.

36.9 We have recommended that Management ensure the proper planning and
budgeting of programs, projects and activities for the LCPC fund to
improve the well-being and cater the needs of the children in the
municipality.

37.5 We have recommended that Management require the Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO) to prepare and submit
the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within the prescribed
period for the timely evaluation of the transactions charged to the fund.
Henceforth, comply with the provisions of IRR of RA 10121 and COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

38.5 We have recommended that Management submit Sanggunian Bayan
resolutions approving realignments, if any, for the expenditures exceeding
its appropriated amounts. Henceforth, ensure the proper planning and
budgeting of programs, projects and activities for the 5% LDRRM Fund to
avoid the noted deficiencies.

39.3 We have recommended that the Municipal DRRM Officer submit
document/s pertaining to declaration/s of state of calamity to support the
transactions charged to the 30% Quick Response Fund.

40.4 We have recommended that Management utilize the 30% Quick Response
Fund for relief and recovery programs in communities stricken by
disasters, calamities, etc.

41.4 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant cause the immediate transfer of the unutilized portion of the
QRF and DRRM-MOOE to a separate depository account of LDRRMF in
the Trust Fund books.

42.5 We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the required
disclosures on DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements including the
unutilized balances in the prior years to come up with more informative
financial statements.

43.4 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended to the Management the
following:
43.4.1 Identify the priority projects to be funded under the 20%
Development Fund with sufficient details and particulars.
Guidelines and policies involving the implementation of these
programs/projects should be considered and included in the
planning and budgeting stage;
43.4.2 Implement only the projects set forth in the Annual Investment Plan.
In instances where other priority projects are identified for
implementation, a Sangguniang Bayan resolution should first be
passed for the realignment and revision of the Annual Investment
Plan to be submitted for approval by the Sangguniang
Panlalawigan, as the case may be; and
43.4.3 Ensure that all priority projects identified by the Local Development
Council be implemented, and funds are fully utilized, in order to
achieve desired goals and consequently elevate the quality of life of
its constituents.

44.3 We have recommended that utilization of the 20% Development Fund be
limited on programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the
attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management
outcomes and shall partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures
and yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

45.5 We have recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer
to transfer all amounts allotted for the 20% development fund to the
depository account specifically created for such fund and the Municipal
Accountant to maintain separate books and subsidiary ledgers for easier
monitoring of utilization and to avoid misuse of funds.

46.5 We have recommended that Management utilize the HGDG in identifying
GAD PPAs which would be the basis in the budget determination depending
on its gender responsiveness or attribute to GAD interventions.

47.7 In the light of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that
Management submit the required SB resolutions pertaining to the
realignments of the fund. Henceforth, have a thorough plan in order to
avoid such violation and ensure that the budgeted PPAs are properly
implemented to properly address the gender issues and concerns of the
locality.

48.3 We have recommended that Management submit the required
reports/documents to COA as support to the GAD Accomplishment Report
submitted.

49.7 We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant, as withholding agents, deduct, withhold and remit the
corresponding taxes on all transactions involving procurement of goods and
services as prescribed by the above-cited provisions to avoid impositions of
penalties by the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR).

50.6 We have recommended that Management:
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile their records on taxes withheld and remitted, and
immediately remit to BIR any noted unremitted taxes;
• Require the Municipal Accountant to adjust accordingly in the books
based on the results of the reconciliation; and
• Henceforth, strictly comply with the provisions of the NIRC and any
amendment thereto to avoid penalties/sanctions imposed by the BIR.

• In view of the noted
deficiencies, we
recommended the
following:
• That the Management
immediately submit for
audit the Disbursement
Vouchers, Contract,
Report of Checks Issued,
and Report of
Disbursement in relation
to the fund utilization of
the DOLE grant; and
• Henceforth, comply with
the requirements set
forth by the COA
Circular No. 94-013.

observations.recommendation:
1.9. In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that the Municipal
Accountant ensure the submission of accounts and financial statements
within the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No.
7160, Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01
and Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006. Monthly accounts should
be submitted along with the journals and financial statements and trial
balances to facilitate audit and verification.

 That the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit the noted disbursement
vouchers to the audit for review;
 That the Disbursing Officer/Accountable Officers refund any unexpended
balance of cash advance at the end of the year or as soon as the purpose
has been served as prescribed under regulations;
 That the Municipal Accountant record liquidation only when properly
supported with liquidation report and supporting documents; and
 That the Disbursing Officer/Accountable Officers regularly submit the
Report of Disbursements with duly accomplished vouchers/payrolls and
supporting documents to the Accountant for timely recording in the books
of accounts for fair presentation of the financial statements.

6.8 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
6.8.1 Management create an Inventory Committee that will perform the
conduct of comprehensive annual physical count of PPE in accordance
with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume 1. Also, a
representative of the Audit Team should be invited to witness the
inventory taking for this purpose;
6.8.2 Management ensure that the Inventory Committee prepare the Report
on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE)
which shall be reconciled with the PPE records kept by the General
Services Office and Accounting Office; and that a copy of the RPCPPE
shall be submitted to the COA for verification not later than January
31 of each year pursuant to Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS;
6.8.3 The General Services Officer maintain Property Cards for PPE and
ARE/PAR and ensure that the said ARE/PAR be renewed every after
three years pursuant to Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish
effective management and control over PPE;
6.8.4 The Municipal Accountant maintain complete and updated PPELC for
each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual for
LGUs to provide ready information on the accounts and to support the
balances in the financial statements;
6.8.5 The General Services Officer and the Municipal Accountant reconcile
their property records with the results of the physical count to ensure
that the PPE recorded in the property records actually exist and all
existing PPEs are recorded in the books, in order to establish
correctness and completeness of the balances of PPE accounts. Any
variance should be handled accordingly and subsequently adjusted if
the need arises; and
6.8.6 The Property and the Accounting Units comply with the prescribed
procedures in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of
unserviceable PPEs, if any.

7.6 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
7.6.1 The Municipal Accountant trace and determine the date of acquisition
of the various assets that comprise the noted PPE in order to compute
for the accumulated depreciation. Further, the computed prior years’
depreciation should be taken up as Prior Year Adjustments to be closed
to the Government Equity
7.6.2 Moreover, we have recommended the following adjusting entries to
reflect the current year depreciation.

Account Debit Credit
Depreciation Expense - Flood Control System ₱1,441,495.44
Depreciation Expense - Water Supply System 801,725.78
Depreciation Expense - Other Infrastructure Assets 943,609.35
Accumulated Depreciation - Flood Control System ₱1,441,495.44
Accumulated Depreciation - Water Supply System 801,725.78
Accumulated Depreciation - Other Infrastructure Assets 943,609.35

8.7 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
8.7.1 That the Municipal Engineer, General Services Officer, and Municipal
Accountant conduct a yearly physical count of local road network
projects and submit to this office the necessary reports and
consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
8.7.2 That the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and
the General Services Officer the description and the cost segregation of
the road components of various road infrastructure projects;
8.7.3 That the General Services Officer maintain a Local Road Inventory
and Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property
Card for all roads and its components;
8.7.4 That the Municipal Accountant keep and maintain a subsidiary ledger
for roads and its components and reconcile the same with the result of
the physical inventory-taking at the end of the year.

9.6 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
9.6.1 The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Engineer, and the General
Service Officer should determine the breakdown of the Road Network
to establish and compute for the depreciation. Also, trace and
determine the date of acquisition of the various assets under the Road
Network in order to compute for the accumulated depreciation.
Further, the computed prior years’ depreciation should be taken up as
Prior Year Adjustments to be closed to the Government Equity.

10.12 We have recommended that Management submit the following verifiable
supporting documents to enable proper evaluation and appreciation of the
COVID-19 response efforts of the Municipality:
10.12.1 Lists of the recipients showing the date of receipt, quantity and goods
received, corresponding barangay and signature, for the distribution
of relief goods worth ₱5,369,200.00;
10.12.2 Distribution documents showing the data on the menu per pax and
how many individuals had been served for the food assistance/ meals
and snacks worth ₱485,166.15;
10.12.3 For the medical supplies, medical equipment, medicines, and test kits,
verifiable procurement documents and distribution lists and/or
Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) supported with
Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS), as appropriate;
10.12.4 Photograph documentations of the COVID-19 response aid and
operations of the Municipality relative to the above expenditures,
properly captioned and dated; and
10.12.5 Supporting documents for the expenditures on the vehicle rentals and
procurement of fuel/gasoline.

11.11 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended that
Management:

11.11.1 Instruct the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the
MSWDO, to submit the MOA along with the project proposal
covering the ESP-SAP, and a copy of the Report of Disbursements
(ROD) duly certified by the LGU Accountant and approved by the
LCE, duly received by the DSWD-FO, for examination; and

11.11.2 Henceforth, all transactions involving fund transfer from national
agencies be fully documented, in accordance with the covering
issuances.

12.9 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that the Management
immediately submit to the audit team the aforementioned required
documents to facilitate its review and analysis.

13.7 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that Management:

13.7.1 Immediately submit to the audit team copies of the Memorandum of
Agreement, SB Resolution and Program of Work or Project Proposal
for the above-mentioned fund received and its utilization for proper
evaluation of the project; and

13.7.2 Henceforth, all transactions involving fund transfer from other
government agencies should be fully documented in accordance with
the COA Circular 2012-001.

15.7 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that the Municipal
Accountant:

15.7.1 Ensure that the amount set-up for RPT Receivables and SET
Receivables at the beginning of the year be supported with list of tax
payers, pursuant to Section 20 of NGAS to establish the correctness of
the collectibles; and

15.7.2 Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor
to establish correctness of the collectible real property and determine
any changes/updates in the real properties.

16.8 In view of the noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
16.8.1 That the Municipal Accountant –
16.8.1.1 Analyze the transaction and adjust the books of accounts
accordingly;
16.8.1.2 Provide adequate disclosure on biological assets in the Notes to
Financial Statements, such as, but not limited to, the following:
(a) Basis of measurement or value recorded in the books;
(b) Description of the biological assets; and
(c) The program under which such biological assets were
purchased and its purpose.
16.8.1.3 Maintain Ledger Card for the Breeding Stock;
16.8.1.4 Coordinate with the Municipal Agriculture Office for dispersal
program under their office, and require submission of duly
accomplished acknowledgement receipt for proper accounting
of animals in the books.
16.8.2 That the Municipal Agriculturist –
16.8.2.1 Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant and submit a status
report and duly accomplished acknowledgment receipt on the
dispersal program of their office;
16.8.2.2 Maintain a Livestock Card for each type of large cattle and other
bovines acquired thru purchase or raised, as well as for small
animals including fowls, as provided under the Handbook on
Property and Supply and Management System; and
16.8.2.3 Reconcile their records with the Office of the Municipal
Accountant to determine the differences and to inform the
Accountant of the raised animals that reached the marketable
age or are ready for use.

17.3 In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
17.3.1 That the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant with
the necessary supporting documents that warrant a Journal Entry
Voucher and General Journal entry; and
17.3.2 That the Municipal Accountant prepare the Journal Entry Voucher,
attached with the necessary supporting documents, as a basis of
recording in the general Journal.

18.6 In view of the noted lapses and deficiencies, we have recommended the
following:
18.6.1 That the Municipal Budget Officer coordinate with the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer in the preparation of the
reconciliation statement of SCBAA;
18.6.2 That the Municipal Mayor instruct the Department Heads to strictly
adhere to the budgetary and disbursement process; and
18.6.3 Henceforth, the Management review the various budgetary,
disbursement, and accounting processes and ensure the strict
compliance thereof.

physical count of local road network projects and consequently update
the local map of the LGU;
1.8.2 the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the
Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road
components of various road infrastructure projects; and
1.8.3 the Municipal Accountant keep and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads
and its components and reconcile the same to the result of physical count
at the end of each year.
1.8.4 the Municipal Accountant disclose the total road network system in the
Notes to Financial Statements

observations.recommendation:
Payments for terminal leave benefits totaling ₱1,092,208.00 were not completely documented in violation of Item 5.13 of the Revised Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001.
Financial assistance was released in the form of aid in crisis situation and donations totaling ₱896,950.00 despite the absence of complete documentation and inadequacy of control measures.
Repairs and maintenance of transportation equipment totaling ₱865,653.25 were not supported with complete documentation and were not properly classified as prescribed under the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Unit dated December 1, 2015.
Meals and snacks totaling ₱1,304,265.21 were paid despite incomplete supporting documents
Purchase of Medicines totaling ₱1,129,652.10 not duly supported.
Purchases of Supplies/Materials/ Equipment/ Furniture & Fixtures with incomplete documentation totaled ₱6,642,793.18
Payments of infrastructure contracts amounting to ₱79,569,289.17 were not supported with complete documentation contrary to Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; thus, the validity and propriety of the transactions could not be ascertained.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:
	
	that management justify the noted lapses;
	that management instruct the municipal officials/employees concerned to submit the lacking supporting documents to COA for evaluation. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant should see to it that payments of terminal leave benefits, financial assistance, repairs and maintenance of transportation equipment, meals and snacks, purchase of medicines and payment of infrastructure contracts are fully supported with the required documentation pursuant to Section 4.6 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.  Meantime, submit the required supporting documents to the COA for evaluation; and 
	institutionalize a non-discretionary determination of amounts given to beneficiaries of financial assistance by setting a predetermined amount for each type of client through the issuance of a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution.
observations.managementAction: The management maximized its effort to submit the required documentations on the commission proper.
The Municipal Mayor has already instructed the Municipal Engineer to closely monitor the projects to prevent the delay of the completion of the said projects.
The management has already reiterated to the Budget Officer to accomplish properly the Obligation Requests and to maintain RAAO’s for proper monitoring of the budget.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the Municipal Accountant strictly observe Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards 1 on the recognition of expenses and liability in the books of accounts to ensure the fair presentation of the school’s financial statements.  She should set a deadline for the submission of all claims before the closing of the books at year end to ensure that all expenses recorded in the year that these were incurred in accordance with budgeting, and accounting policies and standards.

We also have recommended that the Municipality observe the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances provided under COA Circular 97-002, dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already instructed the municipal accountant to be strict in processing claims of municipal employees.
The share from Tobacco Excise Tax under Republic Act No. 7171 was already included in the Annual Budget and there is already an approved ordinance approved by SB.
The Municipal Mayor has already reiterated to the BAC and all responsible employees to adhere the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses especially on the splitting of procurement.  Henceforth, all procurements of the municipality should be conducted through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Section 52 of the IRR as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring entity.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has already reiterated to the BAC and all responsible employees to comply with the said mandate.

observations.recommendation:
Non-capital expenditures totaling ₱904,940.00 were charged against the 20% Development Fund in violation of Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 of the DILG and DBM, thereby depriving the constituents of the Municipality of the long-term benefit that could be obtained from the mandated developmental programs and projects.

The appropriation for one project as included in the approved Annual Investment Plan amounting to ₱600,000.00 was utilized for some other PPAs not included in the AIP without proper realignment by the SB contrary to Section 305 of the Local Government Code of 1991. 

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended the following:

That management strictly adhere with the provisions of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the IRA for development projects. Utilize the fund only for rooted development projects which are permanent in nature.
That management submit justification for the noted lapses especially for the utilization of lumpsum appropriation as well as the utilization of funds without realignment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor has reiterated to the MBO and MPDO to review the budgets and see to it that it complies with the provisions of the said  circular.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to formulate strategies to intensify and facilitate tax collections.  If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of RA 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services. Further, we recommend that the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies to include all tax delinquents and reconcile the same with the accounting records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has already consulted the Sangguniang Bayan regarding on what ordinances to formulate in order to compel those delinquent tax payers to pay their respective taxes.
The Municipal Mayor has already instructed the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balance to the separate depository account.

observations.recommendation:
The GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) is yet to be organized hence the required institutionalization of GAD mechanisms needed to accelerate and ensure the sustainability of GAD related projects was not attained. 

The Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) was not administered/used in the selection of the PPAs to be included in the GAD Plan and Budget, thus, there were no basis in the determination of the amount that could be attributed to GAD interventions.

The absence of review and approval of DILG to the GAD Plan and Budget for CY 2016 casted doubt as to the gender responsiveness of its content as well as its alignment to the identified priorities of both the DILG and Provincial Government.

GAD Code Formulation were not enacted by the Local Sanggunian, consequently the national and international mandates and frameworks on gender equality, women’s empowerment, and women’s human rights were yet to be affirmed at the LGU level.

Various programs/projects and activities implemented totaling                              ₱2,854,858.98 do not fully address the gender issues identified in the GAD Plan. 

In view of the noted observations, we have recommended the following:

that management formulate and adapt a Local GAD Code; 
the MSWDO being the leading authority in identifying gender related issues and concerns, see to it that GAD activities are identified through the use of HGDG, that the GAD plan are regularly updated and that the identified projects do address the GAD issues and concern in the long run; 
Management should follow the prescribed format of GAD plan and see to it that said plan and budget are approved by the DILG and the PPDO; and
henceforth, projects and programs under the agency’s GAD Plan be fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The management already instructed the MSWDO to identify gender related issues.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management exhaust all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing the immediate settlement of the outstanding audit disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Some liquidation for the settlement of audit suspensions have been already submitted and some of them have been already issued with Notice of Settlement by the Resident Auditor. An appeal has been already made by the Municipal Mayor and Vice Mayor for the disallowances issued.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated the following recommendations:
The Municipal Mayor enjoin all concerned officials and employees to strictly adhere with the rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided for under Section 89 of the Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 and 2012-001;
The Municipal Accountant to strictly monitor unsettled cash advances of each officers and employees and send reminders  within ten (10) days before expiration of the prescribed period; and
The Municipal Treasurer liquidate her cash advances for salaries and wages, operating expenses and special purpose within the prescribed period.

We have reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer monitor cash utilization and refrain from using government funds other than for its intended purpose. Moreover, disbursement of government funds should be judiciously planned to ensure the implementation of all targeted programs, projects and activities, and the agency should exercise prudence in the use of the limited cash resources and to stop the practice of incurring expenditures in excess of the appropriations.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have reiterated to recommend the following:
Management exert all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing the immediate settlement of the outstanding audit disallowance in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006; and
The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer, retrace prior year transactions to ascertain the nature of receivables with unknown nature. In the event that retracing of prior year’s transactions could no longer be made due to the absence of the required documents, a request for authority to write off together with a valid justification on the reason that the said amount of receivable should be written off may be submitted for evaluation and proper action.

We have recommended that management undertake the following measures to establish the existence, condition and valuation of local road networks of the LGU:
the Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local map of the LGU;
the Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
the Municipal Accountant keep and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of physical count at the end of each year.

In view of the above noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
that management justify on the noted lapses;
that management submit the MOA with the DSWD, and with the recipients and the lacking documents and request inspection to the COA technical team for proper evaluation; and
that management set a well-defined policy and guidelines on the grant that may serve as criteria in the assessment and evaluation.
Management Comment:
The management explained that the donation was stipulated in the MOA between the LGU and the  DSWD.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The audit team reiterated the submission of the MOA to validate the justification.

We have reiterated our previous audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant exert more effort to identify the sources of the un-reconciled difference between the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds through reconciliation of the accounts and effect immediately the adjustments upon verification.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert effort in locating the documents from prior years to support the validity of said payable and carry out an in-depth analysis thereof as to the amount payable to the provincial government and individual barangay for immediate remittance.
We have also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit immediately the amount due to LGUs to prevent misuse of funds.

We have reiterated our previous recommendation that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards and NGAS Manual on the recognition of expenses and liability in the books of accounts to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements.  She should set a deadline for the submission of all claims before the closing of the books at year end to facilitate the recognition of liability thereto.
Management Comment:
The management decided that a deadline for the processing of prior year disbursements and obligations be set.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher as basis of recording in the General Journal.  The Journal Entry Vouchers shall be accompanied with proper supporting documents before the Municipal Accountant record the transactions.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted the Journal Entry Vouchers.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The Municipal Accountant submitted the Journal Entry Vouchers, however, no supporting documents were attached.

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that:
Expenditures should be limited to what is appropriated. The Municipal Accountant or the Budget Officer should maintain the required registries to prevent the incurrence of expenditures in excess of allotments;
The Municipal Budget should be carefully prepared and reviewed so that the actual needs of the municipality are properly allocated in the budget;
Submit duly approved 2017 Supplemental Budget, if any; and
Obligation Requests which is the basis for recording of obligations in the registries should be properly accomplished to facilitate monitoring of expenses and balances of appropriations.
Management Comment:
Management submitted on May 11, 2018 the SB Resolution/Appropriation Ordinance and justifications that the excess expenditures were charged to appropriations for non-office expenditures such as LDRRM, GAD and 20% Development Fund.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
Management did not prepare and submit Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations. In addition, some of the Obligation Request attached to the DVs do not indicate that the said expenditures were charged to the appropriations for non-office expenditures. Thus, the justification on the excessive expenditures and cash overdraft in observation number 4 could not be readily validated.

We have recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of allowing reimbursements to officers and employees when direct payment is possible to prevent losses and provide reasonable assurance that the transaction is regular.
We have also recommended that management ensure proper and complete documentation of all their future procurements in accordance with the requirements of existing laws, rules and regulations.

In view of the above observation, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately the unsubmitted disbursement vouchers. The Municipal Accountant should see to it that monthly reports and journals are submitted to the auditor’s office together with the paid vouchers/payrolls/official receipts in accordance with the provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and COA Circular No. 2009-006.
We have also recommended that management permanently address the cause of the late submission of accounts and reports by reviewing the existing procedures in the processing of disbursement vouchers and issuance of checks. Provide timeliness of submission of paid DVs and its supporting documents to ensure its submission within the prescribed period.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs to serve as guide in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and furnish COA of the same for audit/evaluation purposes.  No procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP of the procuring entity.
We have recommended that Management use DPWH Department Order No. 197, series of 2016 or its updated issuances on the matter in its succeeding computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects for CY 2018 onwards.
We have recommended that the BAC invite observers in writing in all stages of the procurement process in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) submit written justification/s on the above-noted observation.
We have recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the procedures on receipts, opening and preliminary examination of bids as provided for in the Procurement Manual for LGUs and the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We have recommended that the BAC ensure strict compliance with the requirements of Sections 25 and 27 of the IRR of RA 9184 in relation to Section XI of Appendix 10 on the use of Bid Securing Declaration.  Henceforth, succeeding BSD be in its standard form and duly notarized.
We have recommended that the BAC submit written justification/s on the above-noted findings.  Henceforth, ensure that prospective bidders submit complete eligibility requirements before they are marked as “passed” and declared as LCB.
We have recommended that the BAC and TWG submit written justification/s on the above-noted findings.  Henceforth, notice and execution of award to the bidder-contractor should only be done after the conduct of the post-qualification process.
We have recommended that management see to it that future winning bidders comply with all the requirements set forth under the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 such as the posting of a duly accomplished Performance Bond before the awarding of contract and contract signing.
We have recommended that the Municipal Government, through the BAC Secretariat comply with the posting requirements on all its procurement activities.  Notices of Award and to Proceed, and the approved contract shall be posted in the PhilGEPS website and website of the municipality, if any, within the reglementary period as required by the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
We have recommended that the BAC submit written justification on the above-noted observations.

We have recommended that management explain the noted lapses especially on the splitting of procurement.  Hence, all procurements of the municipality should be conducted through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR, unless justified by the conditions provided under Rule XVI of the IRR as recommended by the BAC and duly approved by the head of the procuring entity.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

We have recommended that management determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to comply with the required submission dates of accounts and reports. We also have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the accounts and reports for audit and the Municipal Treasurer prepare Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Mayor instructed the compliance on the set due date of submissions to COA.

We have recommended that all Memorandum of Agreements entered into by the municipality and Notice of Authority to Debit Account Issued be submitted to the audit team for verification whether the specific purpose was established and met.  Henceforth, ensure that funds received from National Government Agencies and all other sources are covered by Memorandum of Agreement and that the same shall be submitted to COA within 5 days from perfection thereof for audit purposes.

In view of the noted observations, we have recommended that management require the concerned personnel to submit the lacking supporting documents to COA for audit purposes and direct the concerned department head/personnel to submit justification for the noted lapses.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation and in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions and disbursements.  Processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be discontinued.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain Perpetual Records/ Supplies Ledger Cards per stock as provided by NGAS to ensure fair presentation of inventory accounts in the financial statements.  The Supply or Property Unit or the Municipal Treasurer as the case maybe maintain Stock Cards to record all receipts and issuances.  All purchases should be supported with properly accomplished Requisition Issue Slips and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued should be prepared and submitted to COA to support charges made to inventory expense accounts.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and the Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation, adoption and updating of the CLUP and CDP for CY 2018 onwards.

We have recommended that the concerned municipal officials submit the corresponding appropriation ordinance to the audit team for evaluation. We have also recommended that copies of contracts including supporting documents be provided to the COA within five (5) days after perfection. Furthermore, we have requested management to inform the audit team when the projects will be completed so that the team could conduct a timely inspection.

We have recommended that management submit the lacking documents and accomplish the required data in the installation of billboards prescribed under COA Circular No. 2013-004. We also have recommended the submission of a written justification for the noted lapses.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer refrain or avoid cancelling checks when they are not actually torn, mutilated, defaced or with erasures/errors pursuant to rules and regulations of the NGAS. Likewise, submit the other cancelled checks with written justification on the frequent cancellation of checks and for the noted lapses.

In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, thru the issuance of a SB resolution, create a MDRRMO and provide appropriate PS and MOOE fund relative thereto if warranted;
that the MDRRM Designate/MSWDO or other personnel in-charge of the distribution of relief goods submit the required liquidation documents thereof in order to determine whether the goods were actually distributed to the intended beneficiaries;
that management strictly comply with the provisions of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 and the related subsequent provisions on LDRRMF utilization. Personal services, maintenance and other operating expenditures, and capital outlay of the LDRRMO as well as the regular MOOE and capital outlay of the municipality shall be sourced from the General Fund.  The LDRRMF shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM programs; and
that Municipal Treasurer transfers the required unutilized balance of the LDRRMF to the special trust fund bank account.
Management comment:
The municipal accountant submitted the local ordinance creating the DRRMO and list of beneficiaries for the relief goods on May 11, 2018.
Auditor’s Rejoinder:
The audit team observed that the submitted list of beneficiaries’ lack necessary information such as dates received and quantity and unit of goods received.

In view of the above noted deficiencies, we have recommended the following:
that the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point to identify programs, projects and activities that address gender issues such as promotion of gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfillment of women’s human rights and promotion of women’s economic empowerment to be included in the GAD Plan.
that the projects and programs under the agency’s GAD Plan be fully implemented.
that management require the GAD Focal Point to spearhead the  establishment of a GAD database to serve as basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and  policy formulation which will form part of the overall management information system of the municipality.
that management create a GAD M&E Team who shall evaluate outcomes of the LGU policies, programs and projects in accordance with Item 5.1of  the PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC No. 2013-1. The M&E activities shall be included in the GPB.

We have recommended that management  allocate the required appropriation  for the protection of children and establish the required Local Council for the Protection of Children who shall prepare plans and ensure implementation of these in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG MC 2012-120.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer remit promptly all taxes withheld in accordance with BIR regulations. We have also recommended that the management act on the over remittance which is one cause for the cash overdraft that affects the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We have recommended that management exhaust all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing the immediate settlement of the outstanding audit disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, thru the issuance of a resolution, adapt a comprehensive and integrated solid waste management system in the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003 and following the pro-forma ordinance issued by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.
